# Missing databases are rejected unless creation is explicit.
! exec mina serve --db $WORK/missing.db --host 192.0.2.1 --port 0
stderr 'database path does not exist'
! exists $WORK/missing.db

# Explicit creation happens before listener startup.
! exec mina serve --db $WORK/created.db --create --host 192.0.2.1 --port 0
stderr 'listen error:'
exists $WORK/created.db

# New databases cannot be created without applying migrations.
! exec mina serve --db $WORK/unmigrated.db --create --migrate=false --host 192.0.2.1 --port 0
stderr '--migrate=false requires an existing database'
! exists $WORK/unmigrated.db

# Existing databases can be opened without applying migrations.
! exec mina serve --db $WORK/created.db --migrate=false --host 192.0.2.1 --port 0
stderr 'listen error:'

# Omitting --db serves from an ephemeral in-memory accounting schema with a warning.
! exec mina serve --host 192.0.2.1 --port 0
stderr 'warning: no --db provided; using ephemeral in-memory accounting state'
stderr 'listen error:'

# The migrate command applies migrations without starting an HTTP listener.
exec mina migrate --db $WORK/migrated.db --create
! stderr 'listen error:'
exists $WORK/migrated.db

# The migrate command accepts a custom accounting schema.
exec mina migrate --db $WORK/quoted-schema.db --create --schema select
exists $WORK/quoted-schema.db

# The migrate command does not accept HTTP listener flags.
! exec mina migrate --db $WORK/migrated.db --host 127.0.0.1
stderr 'unknown flag: --host'
